title: Ichizhou
content:
The Ichizhou are a notable sapient humanoid species native to the planet [[Airehai]] in the Hydian Sector. They are amphibious, able to survive in and out of water for indefinite periods of time; with the exception of their larval state, which is fully aquatic. They can be easily identified by their elongated heads and bioluminescent spots that cover their necks and back. In addition to being unique identifiers of each Ichizhou, the spots serve a similar purpose to the gills of old [[Earth|Earth's]] many fish species — able to extract oxygen from water for respiratory purposes.

The Ichizhou are renown across the galaxy for their industriousness. Numerous notable megacorporations were originally founded by the Ichizhou; such as the historically notable Lethakos Corporation, and your friends at [[Phodd Communications]]. They are also well known for their sprawling submarine cities: dense hubs of bright lights, culture, and technological innovation that sit far beneath the waves. Thanks to the species' large diaspora, many continental worlds are host to an underwater Ichizhou city somewhere in their oceans.

At multiple points in galactic history, the Ichizhou civilization has been a major contender in interstellar politics. The original [[Ichizhou Empire]] was the first major hegemon in the Hydian sector; and that government's subjugation of the Sarden would lead to that species' eventual independence and acquisition of FTL travel. Under the dictatorial Argonate, the Ichizhou were a major rival of the Union of Free Systems. Eventually, the Ichizhou became willing partners of the union, laying the groundwork for the creation of the Hydian Republic — one of the direct predecessors to the current galactic government.

Physiology and appearance

The Ichizhou are an amphibious humanoid species. They posses webbed hands and feet that — when combined with their powerful leg muscles — allowed them to be adept swimmers. They posses both lungs and a gill-like set of bioluminescent organs known as the Brachi-Iro (loosely translated to mean "color gills") that allow them to breathe in and out of water indefinitely. The arrangement of their Brachi-Iro is unique to each individual Ichizhou, their glow allowing for easy identification underwater. The Brachi-Iro require adequate moisture to function properly, which is typically solved by secreted by the surround skin. However, they are still prone to drying out — causing significant discomfort or even permanent damage — making the Ichizhou poorly suited to arid climates.

Traditionally, the Ichizhou's lives revolved around water, they do not require it, and can thrive in places without it in abundance. Still, they can still be found in the largest numbers in wetter environments and undersea settlements. They are most suited to salt water, but can survive in freshwater indefinitely.

The Ichizhou begin life as fully aquatic, teardrop-shaped tadpoles with short, stubby limbs. They typically transition to their final, semi-aquatic stage around the age of 10, and reaching adulthood at roughly 25. For the first decade and a half of their life, there is little difference between the sexes, but adult Ichizhou display a high degree of sexual dimorphism. Primarily, adult females are substantially taller than males; indeed, standing ~0.2 meters taller than the average human. Males tend to be lankier, with longer arms and oversized forearms for their size. Finally, females' heads tend to be more angular, while males are more rounded.
